🌿 Evergreen Multi-Engine Sourcing System
Improve any engine without redeployment:
• Airtable: exa_queries, competitor_anchors, sectors, naf_codes
• engine_config.json: RSS feeds, Google News templates, caps
• backfill_engine.py: VC portfolio URLs
• New MATCHes auto-seeded into Exa Similar for next cycle.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 27 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 14 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for Profund VC (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. ai_workflow_automation“Here is a French AI workflow automation startup building LLM-powered tools for HR or finance teams in French SMEs and mid-market companies.”
2. ai_workflow_automation“Here is a pre-revenue European AI workflow co-pilot startup integrating with ERP or CRM to automate data entry and approval chains at pre-seed stage.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

Payflows90MATCHPayflows' core business (STEP A) is B2B SaaS for financial operations. This aligns with the 'ai_workflow_automation' angle (STEP B) as it's an AI-native platform for finance teams, automating workflows like procure-to-pay and accounts payable/receivable. The problem, as described in the company's website, applies to both large enterprises and European (including French) SMEs.https://payflows.io

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 30 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 30 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — Profund VC
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 8 companies → evaluated: 7 for Profund VC

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (Profund VC)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

Additionally, the company is based in the United States, failing the European (specifically French) market focus for STEP B.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/novity-receives-st

BravoTran20NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company provides a B2B SaaS platform for accounts payable/receivable automation specifically for freight forwarders, which does not align with either the 'AI-native workflow automation for operations, finance, legal, or HR teams at European SMEs/mid-market companies with France focus' or 'Regulated intermediary fintech for EU with France focus' angles.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/bravotran-receives
Findd10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) immediately fails. Findd is a US-headquartered company focused on workforce management for frontline workers in the US and Canada, which does not align with the European/French market focus or the specific AI workflow automation and regulated intermediary fintech angles of the thesis.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/findd-raises-21m-i
CircuitHub10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Oorja Bio10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails immediately. Oorja Bio is a clinical-stage biopharmaceutical company focused on drug discovery and development, not a B2B software product.https://www.finsmes.com/2026/05/oorja-bio-raises-3
Iron Dome10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. The company, Iron Dome, is Israel Aerospace Industries, a defense and aerospace company. This does not align with 'early-stage B2B software product (SaaS, AI workflow, or regulated-intermediary technology)' as required by the thesis.https://starburst.aero/astra/about-us
Imperagen10NO MATCHINSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE — not enough info on company activity to evaluate STEP 1.https://www.ibioic.com/industry-members-database/i

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — Profund VC
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 2 companies scanned

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 14 total · 2 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

CarTrawler10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because CarTrawler is a global car rental platform focused on travel, not early-stage B2B software for AI workflow or regulated finance. AirAsia Next10NO MATCHAirAsia Next, as described through Capital A, appears to be an airline and a broad digital travel and lifestyle platform, not a B2B SaaS company focused on AI workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech. STEP 1 (CORE business) fails.https://www.capitala.comskift.com/feed
Hopper10NO MATCHHopper is a B2C travel booking and price prediction application, which does not align with the B2B software product criteria in STEP A. It is a consumer-facing product.https://www.hopper.com/careersskift.com/feed
TravelX10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A, B) failed. TravelX's core business is multi-way, dynamic retailing solutions for airlines, which does not align with 'AI-native workflow automation' or 'regulated intermediary fintech' verticals.https://seamless.ai/b/travelx-130137161skift.com/feed
Fairlyne10NO MATCHThe company Fairlyne operates in the travel technology sector, which does not align with either the AI-native workflow automation angle (focused on operations, finance, legal, HR teams) or the regulated intermediary fintech angle. Naboo20NO MATCHNaboo is an AI-powered procurement platform for corporate events, which does not align with the 'ai_workflow_automation' angle focused on operations, finance, legal, or HR teams, nor the 'regulated_intermediary_fintech' angle. Thus, it fails STEP B: the company does not align with AT LEAST ONE active angle.https://thenextweb.com/news/naboo-raises-70mskift.com/feed
Boompop10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Boompop offers event planning and experience management services, which is not a B2B software product in AI workflow automation or regulated-intermediary fintech.https://boompop.comskift.com/feed
Guesty10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Guesty provides a property management platform, which is neither AI-native workflow automation for enterprise back-office teams (operations, finance, legal, HR) nor B2B SaaS for regulated intermediaries (insurance brokers, wealth managers, etc.).https://www.guesty.com/startskift.com/feed
Lexroom90MATCHThe company's core business is an AI-native workflow automation product for legal teams, aligning with the B2B software requirement (STEP A) and the 'ai_workflow_automation' angle since it is an LLM-powered platform for legal workflows (STEP B).https://startups.eithealth.eu/companies/lexroom_aisifted.eu/feed
Jeeves20NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed because Jeeves is a corporate spend and expense management platform, which does not align with the AI workflow automation or regulated intermediary fintech angles, nor does it appear to have a specific focus on France or the French-speaking European market.https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/jeevessifted.eu/feed
Hotiday10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because Hotiday is a traveltech company operating a virtual hotel builder and managing hotel room collections, which is a consumer-facing product/service in the hospitality sector, not a B2B software product for professional or institutional buyers as required by the thesis.https://webflow.hotiday.com/en/about-ussifted.eu/feed
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription
ACTIVE NAF CODES (11, all run daily):

6202A—
6311ZData processing & hosting
6419Z—
6612Z—
7912Z—
8020Z—
8010Z—
8030Z—
8690D—
2651B—

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.
GitHub — Developer Repos (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Searches GitHub repos and organizations for projects matching merged sector keywords. Finds technical founders building in your space before they raise. Free GitHub API, runs daily.

Shared | Rotation: NONE | Cost: $0.00 (free) | Test: 30 leads | Prod: 30 leads
ALL SECTOR KEYWORDS (all daily):
AI-native workflow automation Europe
LLM-powered document processing
agent-based process automation B2B
generative AI enterprise co-pilot
AI-augmented ERP integration
no-code AI workflow builder SME
autonomous approval chain software
AI operations assistant B2B SaaS
insurtech regulated intermediary Europe
wealth management compliance SaaS
KYC AML automation fintech B2B
MiFID II reporting automation SaaS
AIFMD compliance software Europe

Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 27 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod
THIS RUN — 2 queries for sforbt (test mode: 2 of 2 prod queries):
1. hotel_booking_saas“Here is a French or German startup providing a corporate hotel marketplace with negotiated rates and automated booking for mid-market firms with significant travel budgets.”
2. hotel_booking_saas“Here is a Here is an early-stage European SaaS company at Seed or Series A building hotel sourcing and RFP management for travel procurement teams in professional services firms.”
ⓘ No date filter — Exa category='company' does not support freshness filtering. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 60 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 60 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — sforbt
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 8 companies → evaluated: 7 for sforbt

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.
LinkedIn Posts — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ 0 posts this run

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

Per-thesis | Rotation: test=2/day (1/thesis), prod=2/day (1/thesis) cycling through 16 queries | postedLimit: week | sortBy: date | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.10/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (sforbt)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

This engine scrapes TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, and FinSMEs automatically — no sector targeting is possible. The LLM evaluation step is the real filter. To improve precision, refine the thesis gate questions in your fund_thesis.
Crunchbase — sforbt
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 2 companies scanned

Apify Crunchbase → 1 ultra-precise query/day: exact city + exact Crunchbase industry enum + employee band. operatingStatus=Active, companyType=For Profit. Returns 5–25 highly filtered companies. Weekly angle rotation cycles through all angles.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | Cost: ~$0.07–$0.42/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.
RSS — EU Startup Media (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

Shared (evaluated per-thesis) | Rotation: NONE — all feeds daily (cap limits) | Test cap: 50 articles | Prod cap: 500 articles
⚠ Known: some sites obfuscate company names → garbled names filtered before LLM evaluation

Robinhood0NO MATCHRobinhood is a financial services company offering investment and trading platforms to consumers, which does not align with the European corporate travel technology domain (STEP A). Its services are primarily consumer-focused for investing and personal finance, not B2B for travel.https://robinhood.com/us/en/newsroomskift.com/feed
CarTrawler10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails as CarTrawler is a global car rental platform, which is not within the specified core corporate travel technology domains of hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel specified in the thesis. Car rental is ancillary to the core focus.https://www.autorentalnews.com/135881/cartrawler-askift.com/feed
AirAsia Next10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (core business) failed. AirAsia Next (Capital A) operates a broad ecosystem of travel, logistics, and digital services, primarily focused on consumer travel and a specific Asian market. There is no evidence of a B2B software or SaaS offering aimed at European corporate travel technology.https://www.capitala.comskift.com/feed
Hopper0NO MATCHHopper is a consumer travel booking app for flights, hotels, and car rentals, not a B2B SaaS platform for corporate travel managers or TMCs in Europe. It fails STEP A of the gate question as it is a pure consumer travel product.https://www.hopper.com/careersskift.com/feed
TravelX10NO MATCHThe company's core business is in dynamic retailing solutions for airlines, focusing on maximizing profitability of existing inventory, not a B2B software for European corporate travel hotel booking, TMC workflow, payments, or ERP integrations. This fails STEP 1.https://seamless.ai/b/travelx-130137161skift.com/feed
Fairlyne30NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. Fairlyne provides a 'Resale-as-a-Service platform enabling airlines, rail and other travel players to offer ancillary services like flexible tickets or refunds', which is focused on leisure travel flexibility services, not corporate travel booking, payments, or TMC workflow.https://startupintros.com/orgs/fairlyneskift.com/feed
Naboo30NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) failed. Naboo's core business is an AI-powered procurement platform for corporate events, not corporate travel.https://thenextweb.com/news/naboo-raises-70mskift.com/feed
Boompop10NO MATCHBoompop provides event planning and management services, which is not within the European corporate travel technology domain. It does not align with any of the active angles for hotel booking, TMC workflow, corporate travel payments, or ERP integrations.https://boompop.comskift.com/feed
Guesty10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) failed. Guesty provides a property management platform, which is generally for landlords or property managers of short-term rentals/hotels, not specifically for corporate travel booking, corporate payment, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://www.guesty.com/startskift.com/feed
Lexroom0NO MATCHSTEP A failed. Lexroom.ai develops a generative AI platform for legal workflows, which is not related to European corporate travel technology.https://startups.eithealth.eu/companies/lexroom_aisifted.eu/feed
Jeeves10NO MATCHThe company's core business is international banking infrastructure and corporate spend/expense management, which is a generic finance tool, not solely focused on European corporate travel technology. It fails STEP A and none of the specific angles apply.https://www.ycombinator.com/companies/jeevessifted.eu/feed
Hotiday20NO MATCHThe company Hotiday operates as a 'virtual hotel builder' and appears to create collections of hotel rooms for modern travelers, focusing on the supply side of hospitality. While described as 'traveltech' and rapidly growing, its core business as detailed in the description is not B2B software, SaaS, or tech-enabled services that automate hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel. It seems more aligned with a consumer-facing hotel-like offering or a hotel management concept, rather than a corporate travel tech provider.https://webflow.hotiday.com/en/about-ussifted.eu/feed
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 282 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

CompanyScoreVerdictKey reasoningURLSource
JUNK0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (core business) fails. JUNK operates in the restaurant industry (smash burgers and premium desserts), not European corporate travel technology. The company's description and portfolio description clearly state its activity in food service and marketing/communication, which are unrelated to business travel tech.https://www.welcometothejungle.com/fr/companies/juv7_20
Count0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector) fails. Count is a virtual accounting team providing bookkeeping, payroll, and general financial services, not a corporate travel technology company.https://www.trycount.com
Bitpanda0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails as Bitpanda is a financial investment platform for cryptocurrencies, stocks, ETFs, and precious metals, not a corporate travel technology company.https://www.bitpanda.com/en
Greenly10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because Greenly provides carbon management software, which is not within the European corporate travel technology domain. It's a generic sustainability tool, not focused on hotel booking, travel policy enforcement, corporate payment reconciliation, TMC workflow, or ERP integration for business travel.https://startupintros.com/orgs/greenly
Mixpanel Events 2.00NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed. The company's core business, Mixpanel, is a web and mobile analytics tool for tracking user actions, not within the European corporate travel technology domain.https://marketlytics.com/blog/getting-started-withv7_3
Slack10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. Slack is a general-purpose team communication and collaboration platform, not a business specifically within the European corporate travel technology domain. While it can be used for project management or communication related to travel, its core business is not travel tech. It is a generic B2B tool.https://slack.com/solutions/small-businessv7_3
GitHub0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. GitHub is a software development platform, not a corporate travel technology company. The provided descriptions clearly state it is about 'how people build software' and 'help startups go from seed funding to unicorn status,' not business travel or related services.https://www.f6s.com/company/startups.github.comv7_3

Findr: remember everything—DROPPEDAlready in Airtable: Deduped — table: profund_sourcing, base: appC8XYbrR4uWsuZchttps://dot.la/personal-ai-2659213500.htmlv7_3
🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.
Pappers FR — Company Registry (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

French company registry (INPI via Pappers API) → finds recently registered companies by NAF code. 90-day lookback by default. Only covers French companies — useful if your thesis includes French-founded B2B SaaS.

Shared | Rotation: NONE — all NAF codes daily (90-day lookback) | Cost: Pappers subscription

Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

Improve any engine without redeployment:

A. WEEKLY SOURCING MACHINE — Full Calendar

Websets Search — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ IDLE

Websets engine not configured for this thesis. Add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine in Airtable to enable.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Enable Websets: in Airtable, add sourcing_config.shared.websets_engine with enabled: true, count_requested: 25, target_per_run: 5, and 2 engines (ws_discovery, ws_funding) each with a 7-day query_rotation.
Exa Semantic Search — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 27 leads

Exa neural semantic search using category='company' → finds startup homepages matching your “Here is a…” query. No date filter: category='company' does not support freshness. Deduplication against Airtable handles freshness (seen URLs/names are skipped).

Rotation: prod=2/day per thesis, test=1/day per thesis cycling through 28 total | Results: 5/query test, 25/query prod | Cost: $0.008 test / $0.046 prod

1. residential_monitoring_consolidation“Here is a société de télésurveillance résidentielle française proposant des abonnements de surveillance alarme aux particuliers avec centre de télésurveillance certifié.”
2. residential_monitoring_consolidation“Here is a small or mid-size French alarm monitoring company with two to ten million euros recurring subscription revenue facing competitive pressure from Verisure.”

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's exa_queries. Write “Here is a company that [does X for Y customers]” describing a company you'd invest in. Best queries are specific about the customer type and problem (e.g. “Here is a B2B SaaS startup helping hotel chains automate their RFP process”). Runs 2/day prod (1/day test), rotating through all queries over N days.
Exa Similar (Lookalike) — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 20 leads

Exa find_similar() → sends competitor/MATCH homepage URLs and returns companies with similar website content based on neural embedding similarity. Does not support category='company' so some non-company pages may appear (filtered by gate). Competitor domains are automatically excluded from results.

Seeds: 80 competitor anchors + 0 MATCH URLs = 80 total | Rotation: prod=5/day, test=2/day | Cost: $0.016 test / $0.065 prod

4. SureView Systems (anchor)

Deltadore25NO MATCHThe company develops and sells connected-home solutions and energy-management technologies, which includes alarms, but it does not primarily operate as a physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare/personal-emergency response company, nor does it explicitly provide SOC-platform technology for monitoring centers. Thus, it fails STEP A of the gate question.https://deltadore.fr
Gavazziautomation0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. This company manufactures electronic control components for industrial and building automation, not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, telecare/personal-emergency response, or SOC-platform technology as required by the thesis.https://gavazziautomation.com
Entouchcontrols0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (core business) fails. Entouchcontrols focuses on energy management and smart building solutions, not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare/personal-emergency response. It does not align with any of the defined angles.https://entouchcontrols.com
Atralgroup95MATCHSTEP 1 (A) passes as Atralgroup designs, manufactures, and sells electronic security solutions, including a 24/7 remote monitoring center. STEP 2 (B) passes for 'smb_security_expansion' as they protect critical infrastructure, industrial sites, and operate in the French market, fitting the commercial alarm monitoring operator angle. STEP 3 (C) passes as they are based in France. STEP 4 (D) passes as they are an established company with €70M turnover and 112 employees.https://atralgroup.com
Vauban Systems10NO MATCHSTEP 1.A failed. Vauban Systems designs, manufactures, and distributes security hardware and software for physical access control, video protection, and anti-intrusion, but does not offer remote alarm monitoring or telecare services. It sells products to a partner network of installers, not directly providing monitoring services to end-users.https://vauban-systems.fr
Hirschsecure20NO MATCHHirschsecure provides 'high-security solutions' including perimeter intrusion detection, video surveillance, and access control for professional/security markets (integrators, enterprises). This is broadly physical premises security (GATE A passes), but their focus is on providing solutions rather than active monitoring services or a subscriber base for intrusion/fire detection for SMBs/commercial. The company's description emphasizes 'designs and manufactures high-security solutions' and 'integrates security solutions' which is more of a product/integration play and does not align with the 'smb_security_expansion' angle's requirement of an 'operator serving SMBs... with intrusion and fire detection subscriptions, ARR EUR 3-25M, operating certified monitoring infrastructure.'https://hirschsecure.fr
Sectoralarm95MATCHSectoralarm clearly fits the 'residential_monitoring_consolidation' angle, operating as a residential alarm monitoring operator in France with 24/7 monitoring and implied a subscriber base. It also meets the geographical requirement for France.https://sectoralarm.fr
Aplaceformom0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails. Aplaceformom specializes in connecting families with senior care options, which is a healthcare advisory service, not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, telecare/personal-emergency response, or SOC-platform technology.https://aplaceformom.com
Assistly0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A, C) failed. Assistly provides senior care management software, which is not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare/personal-emergency response in the context of alarm monitoring. Additionally, the company is headquartered in the United States, not France or contiguous European markets as required by C.https://assistly.care
Herewith0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because the company provides in-home senior care services, which is not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare/personal-emergency response, nor is it a SOC-platform technology for such services. The company's focus is on connecting families with caregivers, not monitoring technology.https://herewith.com
Seniiors10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (vertical/sector gate) fails. Seniiors provides a cloud-based platform for managing senior care facilities and monitoring residents' health, which is healthcare management, not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare/personal-emergency response as defined. It does not align with any of the specified angles, especially since it operates in the US, not France/Europe.https://seniiors.com
Sagecare0NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A) fails because Sagecare provides AI-native CRM and virtual assistant for home care agencies, which is not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, telecare/personal-emergency response, or SOC-platform technology enabling such services. It also fails C as it is headquartered in the US, not France, Belgium, or French-speaking Switzerland.https://sagecare.ai
Salientsys10NO MATCHSTEP 1 (A, C, D) failed. Salientsys provides video surveillance software, which is adjacent but not directly 'remote alarm monitoring, telecare/personal-emergency response, or SOC-platform technology enabling such services'. More critically, the company is headquartered in the United States and has no clear indication of operations or an established contracted subscriber base in France or contiguous European markets.https://salientsys.com
Omniveillance20NO MATCHThe company provides cloud-based video surveillance systems for businesses, which could align with physical premises security (STEP 1). However, the company is headquartered in Folsom, California, United States, failing STEP C which requires primary operations or HQ in France, Belgium, or French-speaking Switzerland.https://omniveillance.com
Intellisee10NO MATCHSTEP A fails. The company provides an AI-powered public safety and threat-detection platform. While it relates to security, it focuses on real-time threat detection from existing security cameras, which is not the core physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare services sought by the thesis. It is also not a SOC platform for alarm operators or telecare providers, but rather a platform for threat detection at the premise level. Additionally, STEP C fails as the company is headquartered in the United States, not France or a contiguous European market.https://intellisee.com
Dtectr10NO MATCHSTEP 1 failed: Dtectr specializes in AI-powered physical security, which fits the core business vertical. However, the company is based in the United States (Austin, Texas) and there is no indication of operations in France or contiguous European markets, failing part C of the gate question.https://dtectr.ai
Pivotal Vision10NO MATCHThe company provides AI-powered autonomous surveillance solutions for critical infrastructure and military, which is outside the scope of residential/SMB alarm monitoring, telecare, or SOC platform technology for monitoring centers as defined by STEP A. Additionally, the company is US-based, failing STEP C.https://pivotal-vision.com

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add seeds: open Airtable → your fund_thesis → each angle's competitor_anchors. Add exact company names (e.g. “Harvey”, “Ironclad”). Each name is converted to https://www.{name}.com and used as a seed URL. Pick direct competitors in your target niche — broad platforms (SAP, Salesforce) produce too many irrelevant lookalikes. Every MATCH you approve in Airtable auto-becomes a seed in future runs.
Google News RSS — NEXECUR
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 40 articles

Fetches fresh Google News RSS with when:1d filter — always the last 24h. v8 prefers github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases) over sectors_plain for tighter recall. Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article (after title-level pre-filter) and extracts company names mentioned. Runs ALL queries every day (no rotation): freshness comes from the when:1d filter.

Rotation: NONE — all queries daily | Cost: $0.00 (free Google RSS) | Test cap: 40 articles, Prod: 600
Fetched: 40 articles → Gemini miner → 8 companies → evaluated: 7 for NEXECUR
ALL 40 QUERIES — NEXECUR (all active every day):
QUERIES BY ANGLE (40 total · 0/day prod · 0/day test per thesis):
residential_monitoring_consolidation weight=5 — 10 queries
1. “"télésurveillance" AND ("acquisition" OR "rachat") AND France”
2. “"residential alarm monitoring" AND France AND "subscription"”
3. “"centre de télésurveillance" AND ("levée de fonds" OR "cession")”
4. “"alarme maison" AND "abonnement" AND ("startup" OR "PME") France”
5. “"Verisure" AND ("concurrent" OR "rival") AND France”
6. “"alarm system" AND "residential" AND France AND ("funding" OR "M&A")”
7. “"télésurveillance résidentielle" AND ("croissance" OR "expansion") France”
8. “"intrusion detection" AND "monitoring center" AND France”
9. “"alarme connectée" AND "particuliers" AND France AND "abonnement"”
10. “"home security subscription" AND France AND 2025”
smb_security_expansion weight=4 — 10 queries

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Tune precision: fill sourcing_config.shared.github_sectors (short 2-3 word phrases like hotel sourcing, travel expense, vat automation) — this engine prefers them over sectors_plain and avoids the fuzzy-match noise that long 4-5 word sector phrases trigger. Each query becomes a Google News RSS URL with when:1d + quoted phrase + funding terms. Free, runs daily.
LinkedIn Posts — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ○ 0 posts this run

Apify LinkedIn Post Search → scans posts from the last 7 days (postedLimit=week, sortBy=date). Gemini Flash content miner reads each post and extracts company names from the text. 1 query/day per thesis, rotating through all queries.

Per-thesis | Rotation: test=2/day (1/thesis), prod=2/day (1/thesis) cycling through 0 queries | postedLimit: week | sortBy: date | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.10/run

ⓘ postedLimit=week — shows posts from the last 7 days, sorted by date.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add queries: open Airtable → each angle's apify_linkedin_posts_queries. Each phrase targets a different signal type. Best patterns: hiring signal (“hiring VP Sales [sector]”), funding signal (“raised seed [sector]”), product launch (“just launched [product type]”). 1 query runs per day per thesis — the full pool rotates over N days.
LinkedIn Jobs — NEXECUR
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ⚠ API KEY MISSING

Apify LinkedIn Jobs → job postings from the last 24h. scrapeCompany=true returns the company website URL directly. Filter f_CS=1,2 restricts to 1–50 employee companies. A startup actively hiring CFO, VP Sales, or engineers in your niche = strong growth signal. 1 entry/day per thesis with weekly angle rotation.

Per-thesis | 1 entry/day from 28 total · 4 angles rotating weekly | f_TPR=r86400 (24h) | f_CS=1,2 (1–50 empl.) | sortBy=DD | Cost: ~$0.03–$0.08/run

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_linkedin_jobs_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry targets 1 day of the week: keywords (e.g. “CFO hotel technology”) + city (e.g. “Paris”). Best signals: C-level hires (CFO, CTO, VP Sales) = fundraising/growth in progress. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). f_CS=1,2 keeps companies 1–50 employees. f_TPR=r86400 = last 24h only.
Fundraising — EU Funding News (NEXECUR)
↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 17 leads

Fundraising scraper (Apify). Scrapes latest EU funding news from TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, FinSMEs. ⚠ We cannot target a specific sector — the actor always fetches the latest news. Today-only filter applied post-fetch. All results then evaluated by the LLM (which is the real sector filter).

Shared engine (same results for all theses) | Rotation: daily — same empty payload every run | Cost: ~$0.05–$0.20/run | Test: 10 leads, Prod: 30 leads

Avian10NO MATCHSTEP A fails as Avian's core business is industrial AI thermal monitoring, not physical premises security, remote alarm monitoring, or telecare services. 🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add entries: open Airtable → apify_crunchbase_calendar for your fund_thesis angle. Each entry: exact Crunchbase industry enum + city + employee band. Use ultra-specific enums (e.g. “Hotel”, “Legal Tech”, “Electronic Health Record (EHR)”) — broad enums like “Travel” or “Financial Technology” return thousands of irrelevant results. Rotate cities across EU hubs each day (Paris, London, Berlin, Amsterdam, Madrid, Stockholm, Zurich). Country is auto-filled from the city — never add a separate country field.

↻ Daily (all items) ✓ ACTIVE — 50 leads

RSS feeds from EU startup media (Sifted, TechCrunch EU, Maddyness, etc.) → Gemini 2.5 Flash reads each article and extracts company names. Runs ALL feeds every day (cap: 50 articles test, 500 prod). Shared across all theses.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add feeds: open execution/config/engine_config.json, find rss_feeds, and append any RSS URL from EU startup/VC media. No Airtable change needed. Raise article_cap to fetch more per run (test=50, prod=500). To improve precision, add media sources focused on your specific sector (e.g. healthtech newsletters, legaltech blogs).
Backfill — VC Portfolios & Directories (Shared)
↻ 7-day cycle · 1 slot/day ✓ ACTIVE — 282 leads

Firecrawl + Gemini 2.5 Flash → scrapes VC/accelerator portfolio pages and extracts portfolio companies. Rotates through all sources (~9/day). Gemini reads the raw HTML and returns a JSON list of {name, url, description}.

Shared | Rotation: 9 sources/day (68 ÷ 7) | Test: 2 sources, Prod: 9 sources | Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add a VC firm: find their portfolio page URL, add it to BACKFILL_SOURCES in execution/sourcing_v3/engines/backfill_engine.py. Format: "Firm Name": "https://firm.com/portfolio". It auto-rotates into the weekly cycle (total_sources ÷ 7 per day). Test mode scrapes only 2 sources/run to limit Firecrawl costs. Cost: $0.00533/page Firecrawl + ~$0.001 Gemini Flash per page.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add codes: append NAF codes to naf_codes in Airtable shared config (under sourcing_config.shared). Find relevant codes at pappers.fr/code-naf. To change the lookback window, edit pappers_config.lookback_days in engine_config.json (default: 90 days). Current codes are listed above.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sectors: edit sectors_plain in each angle in Airtable — merged across all theses. GitHub searches repos/orgs; HN searches Show HN posts. Both run daily at no cost. Keep keywords short and precise (e.g. “hotel-rfp”, “legal-ai”, “health-compliance”) — specific technical terms that a founder would use when naming a project.
CRT.sh — Certificate Transparency (Shared)
↻ Daily (all items) ○ IDLE

Monitors Certificate Transparency logs (crt.sh) for new domain registrations matching sector keywords. A new domain with “legalai”, “hoteltech” etc. in the name = early signal of a new startup. Free, runs daily (test: 3 keywords, prod: 10 keywords).

Shared | Rotation: NONE — daily | Cost: $0.00 (free crt.sh) | Prod cap: 50 leads

🔧 HOW TO ENHANCE THIS ENGINE
Add sector keywords: edit cert_transparency_keywords in engine_config.json. Use compound words founders typically include in domain names (e.g. “legalai”, “hotelops”, “medtech”, “travelops”). Avoid generic terms like “software” or “tech” — too many false positives. Runs automatically in prod every day.

  ══ LEAD 1/85: OdysseyFather ══
[06:53:12]   URL: https://github.com/OdysseyFather/lingxi
[06:53:12]   Source: github
[06:53:12]   Content (236 chars): 本地优先的全端 AI 工作台。接入 Anthropic、OpenAI、DeepSeek、Qwen、Gemini、Ollama 等 14+ 模型，一键切换。不只是聊天：创建专属 Agent，配备角色/技能/知识库/MCP 工具；可视化编排工作流；局域网内 Agent 自动发现、组网互聊；定时任务自动执行；接入企微/钉钉/飞书；持久记忆跨会话。流式输出、语音输入、截屏、RAG 引用可视化、6 套主题。macOS 双击即用，零依赖。数据全部本地存储。全开源，全透明，全能力。
